text:
Market today: The market mood is mixed. Oil prices are spiking due to Middle East tensions, which could fuel inflation concerns and impact consumer spending. Meanwhile, your core semiconductor stocks are slumping, despite some AI-adjacent areas thriving.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.02%) and SPAL (-41.51%) are in dire straits. Both are leveraged ETFs, meaning their drops are amplified, and they decay over time. Given you're on margin, these drawdowns hurt extra. QQQ (-1.22%), SMH (-10.6%), and QQQI (-2.52%) are also flagged for trimming.

Watch: Keep a close eye on oil price movements and any further news impacting the semiconductor sector. Also, watch for any signs of broader market weakness given the economic outlook is worsening.

Bottom line: Focus on managing your highly leveraged, underperforming positions and consider diversifying slightly given the current market signals.
plays: Your leveraged SOXL is down 44.02% and flagged SELL. Consider trimming or exiting this position to reduce exposure to decay and amplified losses, especially on margin.
This leveraged ETF, SPAL, is also down significantly (-41.51%). Review its stop at 21.38 and consider reducing exposure to this volatile new position.
With oil prices surging, the scanner's high-conviction "ACCUMULATE" on GUSH (11/12, grade A bias) looks interesting. Consider an entry at 35.55-35.52 with a stop at 35.43.
